title: Lucid Trading Rules Explained: Drawdown, Profit Targets & Time Limits (2026)

meta_description: Lucid Trading Rules Explained: Drawdown, Profit Targets & Time Limits (2026). Lucid Trading (4.5★, Free). Every rule explained: drawdown, profit targets, payout
excerpt: Every Lucid Trading rule explained in plain language — drawdown limits, profit targets, trading restrictions, and payout terms.
content:
Lucid Trading Rules Overview

Lucid Trading is a US-based proprietary futures trading firm founded in 2025, specializing in fast payouts and flexible account structures for day traders. The firm offers four account tiers—LucidFlex, LucidPro, LucidDirect, and LucidMaxx—all structured with zero monthly fees and a focus on intraday trading only. Unlike traditional prop firms that charge monthly fees or require capital holds, Lucid Trading operates on a one-time fee model (which rotates with promotions), making it accessible for traders with smaller initial capital.

Lucid Trading distinguishes itself through 90% profit splits, which are among the highest in the industry, and 15-minute average payout processing—significantly faster than competitors who typically process weekly or monthly. The firm supports both CQG and Rithmic data feeds, making it compatible with popular retail and professional platforms including Sierra Chart, Quantower, and MotiveWave. This dual-feed architecture appeals to both order-flow traders and traditional chart-based traders.

Key differentiator: LucidFlex accounts have no daily loss limit and no consistency rule at the funded stage, making them the most permissive option for traders who prefer flexibility over strict drawdown rules. However, all account types strictly prohibit swing trading—positions must close intraday—and HFT (high-frequency trading) bots are not permitted, though general algorithmic and automated strategies are allowed.

Account Types and Pricing

Lucid Trading offers four distinct account tiers, each with different starting balances and fee structures. All accounts charge zero monthly fees, with one-time setup fees rotating based on current promotions. The following table summarizes all options:

Account Type Starting Balance Monthly Fee Profit Split Best For
LucidFlex $25K or $50K $0 90% Flexible traders; no daily loss limit or consistency rule
LucidPro $50K or $100K $0 90% Serious day traders; 100% of first $10K withdrawals before split
LucidDirect $50K $0 90% Order-flow traders; direct exchange access
LucidMaxx $150K $0 90% High-volume traders; maximum profit potential

One-time fees: Lucid Trading rotates one-time setup fees with promotions, making exact costs variable. You must visit the firm's website for current pricing. This promotional rotation is common in the prop trading industry but makes standardized comparisons challenging.

Profit Targets

Lucid Trading does not publicly disclose detailed information about phase-based profit targets or scaling thresholds on standard marketing materials. Most proprietary trading firms structure profit targets in 2–3 phases (e.g., 5% for Phase 1, 8% for Phase 2), but specific figures for Lucid Trading should be confirmed directly with the firm or through their demo account, which is available for free paper trading.

What is publicly known:

  • Paper trading is available (likely as a prerequisite to funded trading)
  • LucidPro and LucidDirect offer a distinct advantage: 100% of the first $10K in withdrawals goes to you before the profit split applies. This means your first $10K profit is yours entirely; profits beyond $10K split 90/10 in your favor.
  • LucidFlex and LucidMaxx profit structures should be confirmed, as the $10K withdrawal benefit may not apply to all tiers

Recommendation: Use the free paper trading feature to understand the specific profit target timeline and requirements before committing to a funded account.

Drawdown Rules

Detailed daily and maximum drawdown limits are not fully disclosed in publicly available marketing materials. However, Lucid Trading's most significant rule variation is the LucidFlex tier, which explicitly has no daily loss limit and no consistency rule in the funded stage—a rare feature among prop firms.

What we know:

  • LucidFlex: No daily loss limit; no consistency rule (largest, most flexible drawdown allowance)
  • LucidPro, LucidDirect, LucidMaxx: Drawdown rules exist but are not publicly specified in standard marketing; typically range from 5–12% daily and 15–25% maximum in comparable firms
  • Reset conditions: Likely daily (resets at market close for futures) but should be confirmed
  • Calculation method: Typically calculated from account equity or balance at the start of the trading day

Key advantage: The LucidFlex no-consistency-rule structure appeals to traders who want to scale risk gradually without meeting strict daily profitability benchmarks. This is ideal for traders whose edge is inconsistent but profitable over time (e.g., event-driven, volatility-based, or discretionary traders).

Traders must confirm exact limits before opening an account, as drawdown rules directly impact trading strategy and risk tolerance.

Trading Restrictions

Day Trading Requirement (Strict):

  • All positions must close intraday—swing trading (holding positions overnight or across multiple days) is strictly prohibited
  • This is non-negotiable across all account tiers
  • Overnight holds will likely result in position force-closure or account suspension

News Trading (Allowed):

  • News trading is explicitly permitted across all account tiers, including high-impact events
  • Allowed events include FOMC announcements, NFP (Non-Farm Payroll), CPI (Consumer Price Index), and other major economic releases
  • This is a significant advantage over competitors who restrict news trading or require news-trading-specific accounts

Automated Trading (Partial Restriction):

  • Algorithmic strategies and bots are permitted, as long as they are not high-frequency trading (HFT)
  • HFT is explicitly prohibited (typically defined as strategies with sub-second order generation or latency arbitrage)
  • Compatible platforms include Sierra Chart, Quantower, and MotiveWave, all of which support bot connectivity
  • Order-flow traders can use these platforms to automate strategy entry/exit

Lot Size and Position Limits: Not publicly disclosed; confirm with the firm to understand maximum contract limits per position or per day.

Weekend/Holiday Holding: Not applicable (futures markets are closed weekends; holiday closures vary by contract).

Copy Trading: Not mentioned in available materials; assume not permitted unless explicitly confirmed.

Profit Split and Payouts

Profit Share: 90/10 (You keep 90%, Firm keeps 10%)

Lucid Trading's 90% split is among the highest in the proprietary trading industry. For context, many competitors offer 80/20 or 85/15 splits, making the 90/10 structure highly competitive.

Special Withdrawal Benefits (LucidPro and LucidDirect):

  • 100% of your first $10,000 in withdrawals goes directly to you (before the split applies)
  • Withdrawals beyond $10,000 split 90/10
  • Example: If you withdraw $15,000, you receive $10,000 + (90% × $5,000) = $14,500
  • This benefit likely does not apply to LucidFlex or LucidMaxx—verify before committing

Payout Processing: 15-Minute Average

  • Lucid Trading processes payouts in an average of 15 minutes—among the fastest in the industry
  • Traditional competitors typically process weekly (5–7 days) or monthly (20–30 days)
  • This rapid turnaround is a major competitive advantage for active traders or those with cash-flow needs
  • Minimum withdrawal amount: Not publicly disclosed; typically $100–$500 in comparable firms

Withdrawal Frequency: Not explicitly stated; most prop firms allow daily or weekly withdrawals. Confirm with the firm.

Payment Methods: Likely bank transfer (ACH or wire); confirm before opening an account if you use international banking.

Scaling Plan

Lucid Trading does not publicly disclose detailed scaling requirements or timelines. In typical prop firm models, funded traders graduate through profit phases or time-based milestones to access larger account sizes. For Lucid Trading:

What is likely:

  • Starting accounts range from $25K (LucidFlex) to $150K (LucidMaxx)
  • Scaling to higher-tier accounts (e.g., from $50K to $100K LucidPro) probably requires consistent profitability, time in the account, or meeting specific profit thresholds
  • The absence of strict consistency rules on LucidFlex may mean fewer restrictions on scaling

Key unknowns:

  • Timeline to scale (weeks? months?)
  • Specific profit target requirements for scaling
  • Maximum account size available to retail traders
  • Whether LucidMaxx ($150K) is the top tier or if larger accounts are available

Recommendation: Contact Lucid Trading directly or use the free paper trading account to understand scaling mechanics before committing capital.

Lucid Trading Rules vs Competitors

Lucid Trading's rule structure is most comparable to firms like Apex Trader Funding, e-Margin, and The5ers. Here's how it stacks up:

Rule Category Lucid Trading Apex Trader Funding e-Margin The5ers
Profit Split 90/10 80/20 90/10 80/20
Daily Drawdown LucidFlex: Unlimited* 6% 5–6% 5%
Max Drawdown Tier-dependent* 12% 15% 10%
Consistency Rule LucidFlex: None* Yes (5 losing days cap) No Yes
News Trading Allowed Restricted Allowed Allowed (with limits)
Payout Speed 15 minutes Weekly Weekly Daily request
Swing Trading Prohibited Allowed (with limits) Prohibited Prohibited
Algos/Bots Allowed (non-HFT) Allowed Allowed Allowed

*Lucid Trading figures marked with asterisks require direct confirmation; tier-specific rules vary.

Lucid Trading's Competitive Advantages:

  • Highest profit split (90/10, tied with e-Margin, beats Apex and The5ers at 80/20)
  • Fastest payout speed (15 minutes vs. weekly or daily-request competitors)
  • News trading allowed across all tiers (Apex restricts this)
  • LucidFlex offers extreme flexibility (no consistency rule, no daily loss limit) for certain trader types
  • Dual data feeds (CQG + Rithmic) support more platforms than competitors

Lucid Trading's Disadvantages:

  • Strict intraday-only requirement (Apex allows swing trading); limits strategy diversity
  • Young firm (founded 2025) with limited operating history; less proven track record
  • HFT prohibited (some competitors allow it with restrictions)
  • One-time fees rotate; harder to compare costs with competitors
  • Specific drawdown and scaling rules not fully transparent

Best choice by trader type:

  • For day traders who want flexibility: Lucid Trading (LucidFlex)
  • For consistency-penalizing traders: Lucid Trading (no consistency rule on LucidFlex)
  • For fast withdrawals: Lucid Trading (15 minutes)
  • For swing traders: Apex Trader Funding (allows overnight holds)
  • For lower drawdown thresholds: The5ers (10% max drawdown vs. Lucid's likely 15+%)

Red Flags and Fine Print

1. Young Firm (Founded 2025)

Lucid Trading was founded in 2025, making it less than one year old. This is the most significant risk factor. The firm has limited operating history, minimal trader testimonials, and no long-term track record of honoring payouts during market stress or firm challenges. Compare this to competitors with 5+ year operating histories. Always research current trader reviews before committing capital.

2. Rotating Promotions and Fee Opacity

One-time fees rotate with promotions, and exact costs are not listed publicly. This makes cost comparison with competitors difficult and could mask higher absolute fees. Always visit the firm's website for current pricing before opening an account.

3. Inconsistent Rule Documentation

As of this review, critical rules such as exact daily drawdown limits, maximum drawdown thresholds, and scaling timelines are not fully disclosed in public-facing marketing materials. This creates ambiguity and requires direct contact with the firm to confirm specifics. Never assume industry-standard rules apply without explicit confirmation.

4. Swing Trading Strictly Prohibited

The absolute ban on overnight or multi-day positions is extreme compared to competitors and severely limits strategy diversity. If your edge requires any position holding beyond market close, Lucid Trading is not compatible with your trading style.

5. Paid Advertising Restrictions

The firm restricts how affiliates can promote accounts through paid advertising. This may reduce affiliate availability and limits your ability to find user reviews or tutorials from independent reviewers. Rely primarily on firm-sourced information and direct trader feedback.

6. HFT Prohibition Ambiguity

The firm prohibits "high-frequency trading" but permits "general algos and automated strategies." The line between these is undefined. If you use bots or EA systems, request written clarification on what constitutes HFT versus allowable automation before opening an account.

7. Verify $10K Withdrawal Benefit Tiers

The "100% of first $10K withdrawals" benefit is confirmed for LucidPro and LucidDirect but not confirmed for LucidFlex or LucidMaxx. Clarify this with the firm, as it significantly impacts actual take-home profits for high-profit traders.

8. Data Feed Compatibility

While Lucid Trading supports CQG and Rithmic, ensure your preferred trading platform integrates with one of these feeds before committing. Non-standard platforms may face connectivity issues.

Final Recommendation

Lucid Trading is an aggressive, high-speed prop firm best suited for experienced day traders who want minimal rules friction and the fastest possible payouts. The 90/10 split and 15-minute processing are industry-leading. However, the intraday-only requirement, young firm status, and incomplete rule disclosure present material risks. Use the free paper trading feature extensively, confirm all rules in writing, and research recent trader reviews before committing real capital.
